BMAD official tip: "Use a different LLM for validation step to avoid model self-validate". Switching model surface inconsistencies same model missed. Especially impactful for AI-driven development.

Cohesion gap = silent killer:

Gap type Symptom in Phase 4
PRD has FR-07, architecture has no addressing componentStory for FR-07 dispatched to Dev, but no architectural foundation → "where do I build this?"
Architecture has FileUploadService, no PRD FR references itOver-engineered component, no clear requirement, maintenance burden
PRD says HIPAA compliant, architecture has no audit log componentCompliance audit fails post-launch, scramble to retrofit
UX-spec specifies focus management, architecture not flag a11y libraryDev picks wrong library, retrofit accessibility 10x cost

Binary gates eliminate ambiguity. "Looks good but..." leaves project in limbo — sprint blocked indefinitely. Explicit verdict (Pass / CONCERNS / Fail) unblocks decisions.

Severity level Definition Required action
BlockerWill fail in Phase 4 if not fixed (concrete failure mode)Must fix before proceed
Major ConcernWill cause significant problems but manageable workarounds existDocument, monitor, mitigate
Minor ConcernSuboptimal but won't block — improvement opportunityBacklog for future

Skeptical scrutiny default: John's PM-as-validator role is question-everything-once. False positives cheaper than missed bugs. Better to flag → human triage → dismiss false positive, than miss real Blocker.

Blameless framing per Etsy/Google blameless postmortem doctrine — focus on system/process gaps, not person blame. PRD has gap = process didn't catch it earlier, not "PM made mistake".

how

Activation: John (PM) invoke via /bmad-agent-bmm-po already IR. BMAD tip: use different LLM for session this if possible.

Configuration Detail
AgentJohn (📑 Product Owner) — _bmad/bmm/agents/po.md
Commandbmad-check-implementation-readiness · short-code IR
Workflow path_bmad/bmm/workflows/3-solutioning-workflows/readiness-check/
Step location6 micro-files: step-01.md ... step-06.md
Output path_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/readiness-report.md

Artifact inventory in step 01:

Required artifact Present? Action if missing
prd.mdRequiredCannot proceed — automatic Fail
addendum.mdOptionalOK if PRD self-contained
decision-log.mdRequiredFail — decisions not traceable
ARCHITECTURE-SPINE.mdRequiredAutomatic Fail
ADRs/*.mdRequired (≥3 ADRs)Fail if <3 — major decisions not documented
project-context.mdRequiredFail — dev agents need conventions
epic-*.mdRequired (≥1)Automatic Fail
DESIGN.md + EXPERIENCE.mdConditional (UI projects)Fail if has UI but no UX spec

Timestamp comparison: architecture newer than PRD = check for drift; epics older than architecture = stale, need refresh.

PRD review checklist in step 02:

Check Pass criteria Common failures
Required sections presentAll sections per Level classification non-emptyExec summary missing, success metrics blank
FR completenessEach FR has ID + title + description + priority + user storyFR without priority (MoSCoW), user story missing
NFR quantificationEach NFR has metric + threshold + measurement method"High performance" without numbers
FR-ID consistencyFR-03 referenced same way throughout"FR-3" in one place, "FR-03" in another
Exec sum match bodyExec summary points all detailed in bodyExec sum mentions feature not in FRs
NFR-FR consistencyNFRs don't contradict FRs (e.g.: real-time vs batch processing)"Real-time" NFR but FR describes batch flow

Architecture review checklist in step 03:

Check Pass criteria
ADR coverageMajor decisions documented: database, language/framework, API style, auth, cloud, observability minimum
ADR qualityEach ADR has Context + Decision + Consequences + Alternatives sections non-empty
ADR status fieldEach ADR has status (Proposed/Accepted/Superseded), not blank
NFR addressedEach NFR from PRD has component/pattern in architecture address it
C4 diagrams presentContext + Container minimum, annotated with protocol/auth/sync
Risk register presentTop 5+ risks identified with FMEA scores + mitigation + owner
Compliance addressedFor regulated domains (HIPAA/GDPR/etc.), explicit components + ADRs

Cohesion check matrix in step 04 — core of readiness gate:

FileUploadService AuthService OCRService SearchEngine AuditLogService
FR-01 Login—X——X
FR-02 UploadXXX—X
FR-03 Search—X—XX
FR-04 AI Q&A—X—XX
FR-05 HIPAA audit————X
FR-06 Notify—————

FR-06 row empty = no architectural component addresses notifications → Blocker. PM/Architect must add NotificationService before Phase 4.

INVEST validation per story in step 05:

Story issue Severity Required action
Story has 0-1 AC definedBlockerInsufficient spec, must expand
Story has >7 ACMajor ConcernLikely too large, suggest split
Story estimate > 5 daysMajor ConcernApply SPIDR patterns to split
Story estimate < 0.5 dayMinor ConcernConsider merging with adjacent story
Story has circular dependencyBlockerRe-sequence or refactor stories
Story missing user story formatMinor ConcernAdd As/I want/So that
Story missing tech contextMajor ConcernArchitect contribute paths/patterns

Cycle detection algorithm — DFS traversal on story dependency graph:

1
Build graph: nodes = stories, edges = hard blocker (A → B means B blocked by A)
2
DFS from each unvisited node; track gray (in current path) vs black (done)
3
If during DFS visit gray node again → cycle detected, output the cycle path
4
If cycle found → automatic Blocker, must refactor stories before proceed

Final verdict computation in step 06:

Count Blockers
→
Count Concerns
→
Apply verdict matrix
→
Pass / CONCERNS / Fail

Handoff:

Pass
→
04·A Sprint Planning

Path 1: Pass → Dev (Amelia) starts Sprint Planning

Needs Review
→
04·A with documented caveats

Path 2: CONCERNS → proceed with explicit risk acceptance, monitor closely

Fail
→
Back to 02·A / 03·A / 03·B
→
Re-run 03·C

Path 3: Fail → PM/Architect fix Blockers → re-validate

Press ESC or click outside to close
Workflow · 04 · A · Phase 04 Implementation · Run by Dev (Amelia) (v6.3+ consolidated)
Sprint Planning
Parse epic files, detect story statuses from file system, generate sprint-status.yaml with flat development_status map. Can re-run to refresh auto-detected statuses.
what

Amelia (Dev consolidated) parse epic files, detect story statuses from file system, and generate sprint-status.yaml — single source of truth for every Phase 4 workflow downstream. Can re-run to refresh auto-detected statuses.

5 steps:

01
Parse epic files — scan *epic*.md in planning artifacts, supporting whole doc or sharded (index.md + section files). Extract epic numbers, story IDs, titles from epic body
02
Build development_status structure — create flat map: epic entries (default backlog), story entries (default backlog), retrospective entries (default optional) for each epic
03
Apply status detection — with each story, check if story file already exists in story_location. If has → upgrade up less most ready-for-dev. Never downgrade status already has
04
Generate sprint-status.yaml — write flat development_status map with metadata header (comment + key:value)
05
Validate + Report — check completeness (every epic/story/retro has entry, has no entry extra, status valid rate, YAML syntax correct), report totals

sprint-status.yaml schema:

Field Type Purpose
generated datetime Timestamp when file is create time first
last_updated datetime Timestamp update recent most
project string Project name from config
project_key string Project key identifier
tracking_system string Tracking backend — default file-system
story_location path Directory containing story files (implementation_artifacts)
development_status map<string, string> Flat map: key = story/epic/retro ID → value = status. Single source of truth for entire Phase 4

Status state machines:

Story

backlog
→
ready-for-dev
→
in-progress
→
review
→
done

Epic

backlog
→
in-progress
→
done

Retrospective

optional
↔
done
why

sprint-status.yaml is artifact-driven state — a core BMAD principle: "Artifacts drive state, not session memory". State persisted in file → every agent read current state reliable:

Without artifact (anti-pattern) With sprint-status.yaml
"I remember we decided X last chat" YAML always authoritative; check file
State lost when session ends State persists across sessions
Different agents disagree on current state All agents read same YAML

Status detection from story files: if a story file already exists in story_location → the story has already been prepared → upgrade to ready-for-dev. This avoids having to enter status manually — the file system is ground truth.

Never downgrade rule: if sprint-status.yaml already has status further along (vd: in-progress), re-run workflow must not reset about backlog. Ensure entire progress actual of team.

Retrospective entry per epic: defaults to optional — reminds the team they can conduct a retro after each epic without requiring it. Status done when the retro is complete.

how

Activation: invoke skill bmad-sprint-planning. Output written into {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml.

Epic discovery in step 01:

Field valid Way process reason
Whole doc exist at Load directly — takes priority over sharded if both together exist at
Sharded (index.md + sections) Read index.md → load ALL section files → combine content
Fuzzy matching Accepts epics.md, bmm-epics.md, user-stories.md, etc..

Story ID conversion in step 01:

1
Header root: ### Story 1.1: User Authentication
2
Replace the dot with a dash: 1-1
3
Convert title into kebab-case: user-authentication
4
Final key: 1-1-user-authentication

Status detection logic in step 03 — with each story key:

Thing event Result
Story file exist at at {story_location}/{story-key}.md Upgrade up less most ready-for-dev
sprint-status.yaml already has status further along Keep unchanged — never downgrade
Story file not exist at, doesn't have status Default backlog

YAML output sample:

# generated: 2026-06-17T10:00:00
# project: clinic-ai-mvp
generated: 2026-06-17T10:00:00
last_updated: 2026-06-17T10:00:00
project: clinic-ai-mvp
project_key: NOKEY
tracking_system: file-system
story_location: stories/

development_status:
  epic-1: backlog
  1-1-user-authentication: ready-for-dev
  1-2-account-management: backlog
  epic-1-retrospective: optional
  epic-2: backlog
  2-1-patient-records: backlog
  epic-2-retrospective: optional

Status enum values:

Applies to Status Definition
Story backlog Only exist at in epic file, doesn't have story file
ready-for-dev Story file already is create in story_location
in-progress / review / done Dev currently do / wait review / complete
Epic backlog Not yet start first
in-progress Automatically action when story first is create (via 04·B)
done All stories in epic achieve done
Retrospective optional Can perform, not start require
done Retro already complete

Validation checklist before writing file:

Check
Every epic in epic files has an entry in development_status
Every story in epic files has an entry in development_status
Every epic has a corresponding retrospective entry
No entry in the status file exists that isn't in the epic files
All status values are within the legal enum
File is valid YAML syntax

Next steps after generate:

  • ·
    Review sprint-status.yaml just is create
  • ·
    Use file this to track development progress
  • ·
    Agents will update statuses during the course of work
  • ·
    Re-run workflow to refresh auto-detected statuses

AI do what (automatically action completely):

Scan all *epic*.md → build a development_status map → detect story statuses from filesystem → generate sprint-status.yaml — single source of truth for Phase 4.

Input from you:

No input needed. Just invoke skill bmad-sprint-planning. Epic files must already exist in planning_artifacts/.

Emphasize ESC or click side outside to close
Workflow · 04 · B · Phase 04 Implementation · Run by Dev (Amelia) (v6.3+ consolidated)
Create Story
Analyze exhaustive all artifacts, extract architecture guardrails, web research latest tech — create story file entire aspect to dev agent implement flawlessly. Run just-in-time for each story, fully automated except step choose story.
what

bmad-create-story create story file entire aspect for each story — exhaustive analysis all artifacts, extract architecture guardrails, web research latest tech — to dev agent has every order need for flawless implementation. Run just-in-time each story, fully automated except step choose story.

6 steps workflow:

01
Determine target story — user provides story path (vd: 1-2) Or auto-discover: read FULL sprint-status.yaml, find entry first pattern N-N-name with status=backlog; halt if has no
02
Load and analyze core artifacts — load epics, prd, architecture, ux (SELECTIVE_LOAD); if story_num > 1: load previous story file, extract dev notes + review feedback + code patterns + problems encountered; analyze 1-5 git commits recent most
03
Architecture analysis for developer guardrails — extract tech stack, naming conventions, API patterns, DB schemas, security requirements, testing standards; identify + read completely files marked UPDATE; document current state / what changes / what to preserve
04
Web research for latest technical specifics — identify libraries from architecture, research: latest stable version, breaking changes, security vulnerabilities, deprecations, best practices; include in story: specific versions, security patches, migration notes
05
Create comprehensive story file — init from template.md at {implementation_artifacts}/{story_key}.md; write complete all sections; set Status=ready-for-dev; SAVE QUESTIONS for end, without interrupting the workflow
06
Update sprint status and finalize — validate story file against checklist.md; load FULL sprint-status.yaml, verify status=backlog, update → ready-for-dev + last_updated; preserve ALL comments and structure; report completion

Story file structure:

Section Content
Story requirements User story, acceptance criteria from epic
Developer context Background, business rules, constraints need know
Technical requirements File paths, API contracts, data schemas, integrations
Architecture compliance Patterns must per, conventions, naming rules, security requirements
Library/framework requirements Specific versions, API usage, deprecation warnings from web research
File structure Files NEW vs UPDATE, exact paths, what changes
Testing requirements Unit/integration/e2e specs, fixtures, mocks necessary
Previous story intelligence (if has) Dev notes, review feedback, patterns established, problems encountered
Git intelligence (if has) Recent commit patterns, file activity, dependencies
Latest tech info (if has) Web research findings: versions, breaking changes, migration notes
why

EXHAUSTIVE ANALYSIS REQUIRED — skill requirement "do NOT be lazy or skim". Each step must read complete, don't skip:

Lazy approach (anti-pattern) Exhaustive approach (BMAD pattern)
Skim architecture, guess patterns Read the entire UPDATE files, document exact state
Drop via previous story intelligence Extract dev notes + review feedback from story before
Use library versions from training data Web research → latest stable, security patches
Ask user midway when need clarification Self-decide, note questions for end — zero interruptions

Just-in-time over bulk-ahead: prep 10 stories upfront → after 5 stories has learnings → 5 stories stale, need rework. Just-in-time ensure each story reflect current understanding.

Previous story intelligence critical to avoid:

Without previous story With previous story intelligence
Reinvent wheels each story Reuse patterns already is approve in review
Repeat again mistakes already encounter Problems encountered → avoid same pitfalls
Inconsistent patterns across stories Code patterns established → consistency guaranteed

Architecture guardrails prevent common LLM mistakes: wrong libraries, wrong file locations, breaking regressions. Skipping read UPDATE files is primary cause of implementation failures and review cycles. Story implementation must leave the system working end-to-end.

Web research ensure dev not use deprecated APIs or vulnerable versions. LLM training data has cutoff — web research fills the gap for latest stable versions and security patches.

how

Story selection logic in step 01:

A
User provides story path (vd: "1-2", "epic 2 story 4") → parse into story_key
B
Or auto-discover: read FULL sprint-status.yaml (ALL lines, don't skip) → find entry first match pattern N-N-name with status=backlog
C
If is story first in epic: update epic status backlog → in-progress
D
Halt if not find see backlog story — notify user clear

Output path:

Config Value
sprint_status {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml
default_output_file {implementation_artifacts}/{story_key}.md
Command bmad-create-story · invoke by Dev agent
Automation Fully automated — ZERO USER INTERVENTION except story selection

Status update flow in step 06:

1
Validate story file against checklist.md → apply fixes if incomplete
2
Load FULL sprint-status.yaml, find story_key, verify current status=backlog
3
Update: status → ready-for-dev, update last_updated timestamp
4
Save YAML: preserve ALL comments and structure — without rewriting the whole file

Handoff 04·B → 04·C:

04·B done
→
04·C Dev Story

Story file at {story_key}.md, status: ready-for-dev — Dev agent read and implement

AI do what (automatically action completely):

Analyze PRD + Architecture + Epic + story before that → research latest docs of libraries → generate story file complete with AC, tech context, architecture guardrails, DoD.

Input from you:

opt
Story ID specific (vd: 1-2). If skip over → AI auto-discover story backlog first in sprint-status.

halt
If sprint-status.yaml not yet exist at: HALT ask 3 options — [1] run sprint-planning, [2] provides story ID, [3] provides path to story docs.

halt
If has no story any at backlog: HALT ask 3 options — refresh sprint-planning, add stories via correct-course, or run retrospective.

Emphasize ESC or click side outside to close
Workflow · 04 · C · Phase 04 Implementation · Run by Dev (Amelia)
Dev Story
TDD-disciplined implementation — Dev (Amelia) load story file, explore codebase, write failing tests FIRST, implement just enough, run full test suite, signal Code Review. Story file is single source of truth.
what

Dev Story workflow is core coding workflow of Phase 4. Amelia (Dev) execute approved story file with strict TDD discipline: red phase first, green phase second, refactor third.

5 steps:

01
Load story + explore codebase — read story file (single source of truth), grep codebase for similar patterns, build context bundle 5-15 relevant files
02
Write failing tests FIRST (red phase) — 1+ test per AC, edge cases + error paths, MUST see all tests fail before writing code
03
Implement just enough (green phase) — code minimum to pass tests, follow existing patterns, no speculative features; refactor after green
04
Full test suite + quality gates — run all tests (unit + integration + e2e), check coverage ≥ threshold, no new lint/type/security warnings
05
Update status + handoff to TEA — sprint-status: in-progress → review, commit message [story-id] verb + object, signal Code Review 04·D

Context bundle structure:

Component Source
Story file SM-prepared, contains AC + tech context + DoD
Project conventions project-context.md — patterns, naming, anti-patterns
Relevant code files 5-15 files identified via grep — existing patterns to extend
API contracts OpenAPI/SDL/proto references in tech context
Architecture references Specific ADRs mentioned in tech context
why

TDD red-green-refactor (Kent Beck) is discipline core of Amelia's work:

Phase What happens Why it matters
Red Write failing tests from AC, run, see them fail Articulate behavior before implementation; if can't write test = AC still vague, caught here not in review
Green Write minimum code to make tests pass Just enough = no speculative features; YAGNI honored
Refactor Clean up code (DRY, SOLID, naming) while tests still green Safe — tests catch regression; improve structure with confidence

Red phase confirmation mandatory: MUST see all tests fail before writing code. Common anti-pattern: write tests, immediately run, tests pass — possibly because:

False pass cause Detection
Test has no assertion (forgot expect/assert) Test file syntax check
Test file not actually loaded by runner Verify test count increased
Test setup creates state that bypasses requirement Code review of test setup

Without red phase confirmation, "tests pass" = false positive — code never actually verified.

Story file = SINGLE source of truth. If conflict between story and any other source, story wins. Dev not re-read PRD/architecture/epics each cycle — story is self-contained:

Re-read everything (anti-pattern) Story-only (BMAD pattern)
Context window bloated with PRD + architecture + epics + story Focused context — only story file
Conflicts between sources confuse implementation Story is canonical, conflict = SM problem to resolve
Slow per-cycle Fast — load only what needed

Never lie about tests = trust foundation. If tests fail, fix or escalate — never claim passing if not. AI agents that lie about test status = system collapse, reviews lose meaning, regressions ship undetected.

Just enough implementation:

Over-engineering (anti-pattern) Just enough (BMAD pattern)
Add abstraction for hypothetical future Concrete code for current AC; refactor when 3rd use case appears
Configurable everything Hardcode until variation needed
"Just in case" parameters YAGNI — add when actual requirement exists
Premature DRY (extract on 2nd duplicate) Rule of 3 — refactor on 3rd occurrence

Full suite over local: run all tests (unit + integration + e2e), not just new tests. Local change can break unrelated code through:

Mechanism Example
Shared utility modified Updated date formatter, breaks legacy display elsewhere
Interface change Added required param, callers in other modules fail
Side effect Modified global state, parallel test interference
Dependency upgrade Library new version subtly breaks dependent code

A few seconds to run the full suite << hours debugging post-ship regression.

Commit message format: [story-id] verb + object — full traceability from requirement → AC → test → code → commit:

FR-03 in PRD
→
Story epic-02-S3
→
AC-1 in story
→
test file
→
code file
→
[epic-02-S3] add upload service

Audit-ready: any commit traceable back to business requirement.

how

Activation: Amelia (Dev) invoke via /bmad-agent-bmm-dev already DS. Auto-suggested when story file ready from SM.

Configuration Detail
Agent Amelia (💻 Developer Agent) — _bmad/bmm/agents/dev.md
Command bmad-dev-story · short-code DS
Workflow path _bmad/bmm/workflows/4-implementation-workflows/dev-story/
Step location 5 micro-files: step-01.md ... step-05.md
Output Code commits + updated sprint-status.yaml

Codebase exploration protocol in step 01:

1
Read story file completely — AC, tech context, DoD
2
Read project-context.md — conventions, anti-patterns
3
tree / glob — understand project structure
4
grep / ripgrep — find similar patterns (e.g. "find existing Repository implementations")
5
Identify: existing utilities to reuse, related tests, relevant modules
6
Build context bundle: 5-15 file paths + 1-line summary each

Test writing protocol in step 02 — AAA pattern:

describe('upload-service', () => {
  it('AC-1: uploads PDF ≤ 10MB successfully', async () => {
    // ARRANGE
    const mockS3 = jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({ ETag: 'abc' });
    const file = new File([new Uint8Array(5_000_000)], 'doc.pdf');
    const service = new UploadService(mockS3);

    // ACT
    const result = await service.upload(file, userId);

    // ASSERT
    expect(result.status).toBe('uploaded');
    expect(result.ocrTriggered).toBe(true);
    expect(mockS3).toHaveBeenCalledWith(expect.objectContaining({ Bucket: 'insurance-docs-prod' }));
  });
});

Red phase verification: run tests, MUST see them all fail. If any unexpectedly pass:

Symptom Investigation
Test passes immediately Check for missing assertion, wrong file path, test runner not picking up file
Test skipped silently Verify describe.only / it.skip not present
Test setup creates passing state Review fixtures — should fail before implementation

Implementation principles in step 03:

Principle Application
Follow existing patterns Use Repository pattern from auth-service.ts (mentioned in tech context)
Reuse utilities Don't re-implement AuthService.getCurrentUser() — call existing
Clean code Meaningful names, small functions, no magic numbers
SOLID Single responsibility per class/function, open/closed where extensibility needed
KISS Keep It Simple Stupid — no clever code unless necessary
YAGNI You Aren't Gonna Need It — no speculative features

Refactor cycle after green:

A
Tests green ✓ — implementation works
B
Identify smells — duplicate code, long functions, unclear names
C
Refactor incrementally — small changes, run tests each time
D
Tests still green ✓ — refactor regression-safe

Quality gates checklist in step 04:

Gate Command Threshold
Full test suite npm test / pytest 100% pass — no failing tests
Coverage npm run coverage ≥ 80% for new code
Lint npm run lint 0 errors, 0 warnings on changed files
Type check npm run typecheck 0 type errors
Security scan npm audit / Snyk 0 high/critical vulnerabilities

Flaky test investigation: if test sometimes fails, run 3x. Random failure = flaky → must fix before ship (intermittent prod issue otherwise).

Atomic status update in step 05:

# Before
- id: epic-02-S3
  status: in-progress

# After Amelia commits
- id: epic-02-S3
  status: review
  completion_timestamp: 2026-04-08T14:32:00Z

Commit message format:

Bad (anti-pattern) Good (BMAD pattern)
fix stuff [epic-02-S3] add upload service
WIP [epic-02-S3] handle PDF max size boundary
final version [epic-02-S3] add network resume for uploads

Handoff:

04·C done
→
04·D Code Review

Status: review → Dev (Amelia — Quinn QA absorbed) picks up

AI do what (automatically action completely):

Read story file → write failing tests (Red) → implement minimum code (Green) → refactor → run full test suite → commit → update sprint-status.yaml into review. Run related continue without stopping except when encounter HALT condition.

Input from you:

opt
Story file path specific. Drop via → AI auto-discover story ready-for-dev first.

halt
If not find see story ready-for-dev: HALT ask 4 options — [1] run create-story, [2] run validate-create-story, [3] specify story path, [4] view sprint-status.

halt
While implementing: HALT if needing to add dependencies outside spec, 3 consecutive related failures, or missing required starting config.

opt
End workflow (step 10): AI ask you has need explain about implementation, technical decisions, or patterns already use not.

Emphasize ESC or click side outside to close
Workflow · 04 · I · Phase 04 Implementation · Run by Dev (Amelia)
QA · E2E Tests
Create API and E2E tests for implemented code — detect framework, identify features, generate tests, run + fix, create summary.
what

QA E2E Tests workflow help Amelia generate automated API and E2E tests for implemented features — not TDD (tests is write after code already ship). 5 steps: detect framework → identify features → generate tests → run tests → create summary.

01
Detect framework — check package.json dependencies + existing test files. If doesn't have framework: analyze project type → suggest + confirm with user
02
Identify features — ask user want test what: feature/component name, directory to scan, or auto-discover
03
Generate tests — API: status codes (200/400/404/500) + happy path + 1-2 error cases; E2E: semantic locators (roles/labels/text), linear flows, visible outcomes
04
Run tests — execute with project's test command; if has failures → fix immediately
05
Create summary — markdown summary: Generated Tests + Coverage + Next Steps → save to tests/test-summary.md

Analyze distinct test types in workflow this:

Type Tool Scope
API tests Supertest / Axios / project's framework HTTP request → response validation
E2E tests Playwright / Cypress User journey from browser to DB
why

Test coverage for existing features is usually low because code was written before tests — Quick Dev doesn't enforce TDD. This workflow fills that gap:

  • ·
    Regression safety: features has no test = not ai know they break when refactor. Tests do explicit contract
  • ·
    CI gate: E2E/API tests in CI pipeline = deployment confidence. Has no tests = merge-to-main is leap of faith
  • ·
    Living documentation: well-named tests model describe expected behavior — new into team read tests understand product nhanh more read docs
  • ·
    Fit into existing CI: detect framework before = tests use correct tool, doesn't need setup add pipeline separate
how
  • ·
    Detect: check package.json devDependencies; has playwright → use Playwright; jest/vitest → use immediately; has no → search online for stack that
  • ·
    E2E locators: getByRole('button', {name: 'Submit'}), getByLabel('Email'), getByText('Success') — Not use data-testid
  • ·
    API: test status codes 200/400/404/500; happy path + 1-2 error cases; validate response structure
  • ·
    Run: use project's test command; failures → fix immediately, don't skip

AI do what (partial auto):

Detect test framework from package.json → generate API tests (200/400/404/500) + E2E tests (semantic locators) → execute tests → fix failures immediately → write test-summary.md.

Input from you:

01 · halt
Feature name, directory to scan, or say "auto-discover". This is the only step that needs input.

opt
Confirm framework if AI not auto-detect is from package.json. Use after Quick Dev — fills the coverage gap since Quick Dev doesn't enforce TDD.

Workflow · 04 · D · Phase 04 Implementation · Run by Dev (Amelia)
Code Review
Adversarial review — spawn 3 parallel subagents (Blind Hunter / Edge Case Hunter / Acceptance Auditor) with context isolation; triage findings into 4 buckets; log into story file; update sprint-status.yaml.
what

Code Review workflow is adversarial gate before merge. Skill run per step-file architecture — 4 step-files independent, load week automatically, don't skip.

4 steps:

01
Gather context — 5-tier cascade find review target (story file → PR → branch arg → default branch → HALT); determine full or no-spec mode; build git diff
02
Spawn 3 parallel subagents — Blind Hunter (diff only) + Edge Case Hunter (diff + project read) + Acceptance Auditor (only when full mode); fallback: generate prompt files if has no subagents
03
Triage findings — normalize → deduplicate → classify into 4 buckets: decision_needed / patch / defer / dismiss; drop dismiss items
04
Present, resolve & update status — log into story file; HALT for decision_needed; handle patches; update story status → done or in-progress; update sprint-status.yaml

3 subagents — orthogonal coverage:

Subagent Context received Hunting for
Blind Hunter Code only, NO spec/AC Logic errors, runtime exceptions, security issues — unintended behaviors
Edge Case Hunter Code only, system map Boundary errors, race conditions, null/empty/overflow — all paths traced
Acceptance Auditor Story AC + code AC drift, missing error handling, incomplete flows — spec compliance

4 triage buckets (step 03):

Bucket Thing event Action next
decision_needed Ambiguous — AI not automatically decide is; only has when full mode HALT → user decide (into patch / defer / dismiss)
patch Fix clear, doesn't need human input Apply all / leave as action items / walk through each
defer Pre-existing issue, not do change this cause ra Ghi into deferred-work.md, not block story
dismiss Noise / false positive Drop, log count in summary
why

AI rubber-stamps own work unless explicitly instructed otherwise. Adversarial mandate forces genuine analysis — Amelia must find issues, not default-approve.

Without adversarial pattern With adversarial pattern
AI reviews own code, finds it acceptable AI instructed to find problems
"Code looks good" — blind spot persists Issues surfaced, human triage filters
Quality drift over time Quality maintained via discipline
Single reviewer perspective 3 orthogonal perspectives — comprehensive coverage

Multi-perspective orthogonality — single reviewer miss ≥1 dimension:

Reviewer perspective What it catches uniquely
Blind Hunter (no anchoring) Logic bugs that "look right" because story made them plausible. Without spec, fresh eyes catch unintended behaviors.
Edge Case Hunter (path tracer) Boundary errors, race conditions in concurrency, paths never tested. Systematic branch traversal.
Acceptance Auditor (spec checker) AC drift — code does X, AC says Y. Missing error handling explicit in AC.

Context isolation enforces orthogonality:

Without isolation (anchoring bias) With isolation (BMAD pattern)
Blind Hunter sees story → assumes code reflects intent Blind Hunter literally receives no spec → judges code on its merits
"Story says X, code does X, looks good" "Code does Y. Is Y correct? Investigate."
Confirmation bias contaminates review Fresh perspective catches unintended behaviors

v6.x fix: no minimum findings requirement. Earlier versions forced minimum 3 findings → fabricated issues when code clean:

Forced minimum (anti-pattern) v6.x improved (BMAD pattern)
Must find ≥3 issues → fabricate trivial ones 0 findings OK if code genuinely clean
Dev fixes nonsense findings → waste time Dev's time respected
Review becomes noise → ignored Review meaningful → respected

4-bucket triage eliminates ambiguity. Each bucket has action clear — has no "Blocker/Major/Minor" must tranh reasoning has block or not:

Comparison Severity ranking (old) 4-bucket triage (BMAD)
What blocks merge? Debatable — Major has block not? decision_needed not yet resolve → in-progress
Pre-existing issue Still is Blocker/Major Defer → deferred-work.md, not block story
False positive Minor but still cost time process reason dismiss → drop, only log count

False positives are feature, not bug. Adversarial pattern will over-find — instructed to. Triage filters signal from noise. Better to over-flag than miss real bug.

decision_needed findings → HALT, user decide. patch → user choose apply all / walk through / leave as action items. Status done only set when all resolved — not to story in limbo.

how

Activation: Amelia (Dev — Quinn QA absorbed) invoke via /bmad-agent-bmm-tea already CR. Auto-triggered after Dev finishes (status: review).

Configuration Detail
Agent Amelia (💻 Dev consolidated) — _bmad/tea/agents/tea.md
Command bmad-code-review · short-code CR
Workflow path _bmad/tea/workflows/code-review/
Architecture 4 step-files (step-01 → step-04), load week automatically
Output Story file (Review Findings section) + deferred-work.md + sprint-status.yaml

Review mode detection in step 01:

Mode Thing event Affect effect
full Find see story file (Tier 1-3 cascade) Acceptance Auditor run; decision_needed bucket available
no-spec Not find see story file after 5 tiers Acceptance Auditor skip; decision_needed reclassified → patch/defer

Subagent spawning in step 02 — 3 parallel, context-isolated:

Blind Hunter prompt:
"You are reviewing code. You have NO context about what this code is supposed to do. Read each function, judge it on its own merits. Hunt for: logic errors, security vulnerabilities, runtime exceptions, off-by-one errors. Report findings with file:line."

Edge Case Hunter prompt:
"You are tracing all branching paths in this code. For each if/else, try/catch, early return — what inputs lead there? Look for: boundary errors, race conditions, null/empty/overflow scenarios. Report findings with file:line."

Acceptance Auditor prompt:
"Story acceptance criteria are: [AC list]. Does the implementation address each AC? Look for: AC drift, missing error paths, incomplete flows. Report findings with AC-id + file:line."

All 3 run simultaneously. Each returns structured finding list: location + type + description + severity.

Triage pipeline in step 03:

1
Normalize — convert 3 output formats (Blind Hunter: markdown; Edge Case: JSON; Auditor: markdown) → unified: id, source, title, detail, location
2
Deduplicate — together issue from many reviewers → merge, set source = "blind+edge" etc..
3
Classify → decision_needed / patch / defer / dismiss; drop dismiss

Story file output format (step 04):

## Review Findings
- [ ] [Review][Decision] title — decision_needed, wait human
- [ ] [Review][Patch] title — patch, wait apply
- [x] [Review][Defer] title — log into deferred-work.md
dismiss items dropped, count logged in summary

Status update end step 04:

State after triage Status update
All resolved (or clean review) review → done
Still action items (patch/decision not yet process reason) review → in-progress

Handoff:

status → done
→
04·B Create Story (next)

Path 1: all resolved → sprint continues, create next story

status → in-progress
→
04·C Dev Story (fix & re-run)

Path 2: still action items → Dev fix, re-run code-review

End of epic
→
04·F Retrospective

Path 3: Last story of epic done → SM trigger epic retrospective

AI do what (mostly auto):

Spawn 3 subagents in parallel (Blind Hunter, Edge Case Hunter, Acceptance Auditor) → analyze type findings into patch / defer / dismiss automatically action → apply patches and log deferred items.

Input from you:

→
Doesn't need what before run — trigger with CR or auto-trigger after 04·C.

halt
When subagent find ra finding decision_needed (not sure is bug real or false positive): you choose patch / defer / dismiss.
If has no decision_needed findings, workflow complete doesn't need input any.

Emphasize ESC or click side outside to close
Workflow · 04 · E · Phase 04 Implementation · Run by Dev (Amelia)
Correct Course
Manage significant changes during sprint execution — Amelia (Dev) analyze impact via checklist.md (6 sections), draft change proposals old→new, generate Sprint Change Proposal document, route Minor→Dev / Moderate→PO+Dev / Major→PM+Architect.
what

Correct Course is the workflow for Amelia (Dev) to handle significant changes during the sprint — analyzing impact via checklist.md (6 sections), drafting proposals in old → new format, then producing a Sprint Change Proposal document to route to the right approver.

6 steps:

01
Initialize — confirm trigger, verify PRD + Epics (HALT if missing), choose Incremental or Batch mode
02
Analyze change — run checklist.md 6 sections, mark mark [x] / [N/A] / [!] for each item
03
Draft proposals — write each proposal per format old → new; Incremental mode: [A]pprove / [E]dit / [S]kip per proposal
04
Generate Sprint Change Proposal — total valid 5 sections into file sprint-change-proposal-{date}.md
05
Finalize & route — analyze type Minor / Moderate / Major → route correct approver, update sprint-status.yaml
06
Complete — summary deliverables, confirm handoff

3 change paths (from checklist.md §4):

Path When any use
Direct Adjustment Change small, update artifact directly that doesn't need rollback
Potential Rollback Change large more, can need revert some stories already do
MVP Review Change affect effect scope total can, need re-evaluate MVP boundaries

Routing per scope:

Scope Route to
Minor Dev automatically approve & implement
Moderate PO + Dev review & approve
Major PM + Architect review & approve
why

Mid-sprint changes happen regularly. Without a clear workflow, a team can easily silently absorb a change or accidentally miss a conflict between artifacts.

Has no Correct Course With Correct Course (BMAD)
Change must not document → lose traceable Sprint Change Proposal keep again decision + rationale
Not know route ai to approve Minor/Moderate/Major routing clear
Conflict between PRD/Architecture/UX is skip over checklist.md §3 check artifact conflicts has system
Epic impact must not mark value complete checklist.md §2 Epic Impact Assessment start require

checklist.md 6 sections ensure without missing:

§ Section
1 Understand Trigger & Context
2 Epic Impact Assessment
3 Artifact Conflict & Impact Analysis (PRD / Architecture / UX / other)
4 Path Forward Evaluation (Direct Adjustment / Potential Rollback / MVP Review)
5 Sprint Change Proposal Components
6 Final Review & Handoff (update sprint-status.yaml for approved epic changes)

Sprint Change Proposal = traceable decision record: who decided what, why, and which artifacts changed — helps the team avoid repeating past mistakes and lets a later reviewer understand enough context.

how

Activation: Amelia (Dev) invoke skill bmad-correct-course. Need has PRD and Epics — HALT if missing a in hai. Architecture and UX Design is optional.

Input Start require?
PRD Start require (HALT if missing)
Epics Start require (HALT if missing)
Architecture Optional
UX Design Optional

Two working modes:

Mode Way operation
Incremental Each proposal output current → user [A]pprove / [E]dit / [S]kip → continue continue
Batch Draft all proposals → present the entire → user review a time

Format proposal (step 03):

Artifact: PRD — Section 3.2 Authentication
Old: "Users authenticate via username/password"
New: "Users authenticate via OAuth 2.0 (Google/GitHub)"
Reason: Stakeholder requirement change discovered in sprint

Output file:

checklist.md analysis
→
old→new proposals
→
sprint-change-proposal-{date}.md

Save into {planning_artifacts}/sprint-change-proposal-{date}.md

Routing after approve:

Minor
→
Dev automatically implement
Moderate
→
PO + Dev review
Major
→
PM + Architect review

AI do what (interactive):

Analyze impact via checklist 6 sections → draft each proposal per format old → new → total valid Sprint Change Proposal → route to correct approver (Minor / Moderate / Major).

Input from you:

01 · halt
Describe the trigger: what change is currently happening. AI asks "What specific issue or change has been identified?"

02 · halt
Choose mode: Incremental (decision each proposal immediately) or Batch (view all already review a time).

03 · halt ×n
If Incremental: with each proposal, you choose [a]pprove / [e]dit / [s]kip. HALT many time.

04 · halt
After generate Sprint Change Proposal: HALT ask Continue [c] or Edit [e].

05 · halt
Final approval: HALT ask clear yes / no / revise before route implementation.

Emphasize ESC or click side outside to close
Workflow · 04 · F · Phase 04 Implementation · Run by Dev (Amelia) (v6.3+ consolidated)
Epic Retrospective
Per-epic retrospective — two-part format: (1) Epic Review: deep story analysis from story files + party mode discussion blameless; (2) Next Epic Preparation: preview, significant change detection, critical readiness. Save epic-{N}-retro-{date}.md, update sprint-status.
what

Retrospective workflow run per epic completed (not per sprint like Scrum pure). Two-part format: (1) Epic Review — deep story analysis from story files + party mode discussion blameless; (2) Next Epic Preparation — preview epic {N+1}, significant change detection, critical readiness check. Amelia (Dev) facilitate with user is active participant.

7 steps:

01
Epic discovery — load sprint-status, find epic cao most has stories done, HALT confirm with user, verify completion
02
Deep story analysis — read Each story file, extract dev notes / review feedback / lessons / tech debt / testing insights → synthesize cross-story patterns
03
Previous retro integration — load epic-{N-1}-retro-*.md, cross-reference action items ✅/⏳/❌, assess follow-through
04
Next epic preview — load epic {N+1}, identify dependencies, gaps, prep tasks, check prerequisites
05
Epic review discussion (party mode) — Amelia facilitates blameless discussion, multiple WAIT points for user, what went well + friction + previous follow-through
06
Next epic prep + action items — SMART action items, prep tasks (critical/parallel/nice-to-have), significant change detection 🚨, critical readiness check
07
Save & update status — save epic-{N}-retro-{date}.md, update sprint-status: epic-{N}-retrospective → done

Retro file structure (epic-{N}-retro-{date}.md):

Section Content
Epic summary + metrics Participants, story count, completion status
Successes What went well with specific story-file evidence
Challenges Friction points, blameless framing
Key insights Cross-story patterns, breakthroughs, lessons
Previous retro follow-through Action item ✅/⏳/❌ status
Next epic prep Gaps, critical path, significant change flag
Action items SMART with owner + success criteria
Readiness assessment Production ready? Stakeholder accepted? Blockers?
why

Per-epic not per-sprint: BMAD's design choice. Reasons:

Per-sprint retro (Scrum pure) Per-epic retro (BMAD)
Every 2 weeks ceremony Every 2-6 weeks (per epic completion)
Same friction discussed repeatedly Aggregate enough data for meaningful patterns
Action items pile up unaddressed Max 3 actions, focused, achievable
Ceremony fatigue Meaningful when epic-sized chunk done

Data-based retro = actionable retro. Without facts, retro devolves into venting session:

Opinion-only retro (anti-pattern) Data-based retro (BMAD pattern)
"Sprint felt slow" "Cycle time increased 40% from sprint 1 to sprint 3"
"Reviews took forever" "45% of stories needed > 1 review iteration"
"Communication was bad" "Cross-team blockers caused 5 day delay across 3 stories"
Emotional, not actionable Quantified — comparable across epics, actionable

Deep story analysis thay because memory:

Memory-based retro (anti-pattern) Story-file-based retro (BMAD pattern)
Team recalls whatever they happen to remember Read Each story file — dev notes, review feedback, lessons
Recent bias — only remember stories end Work with — all stories is analyze
Vague opinions Evidence from story files — specific, traceable

Party mode = psychological safety:

Blame framing (anti-pattern) Party mode blameless (BMAD pattern)
"Why did Alex miss the deadline?" "Why did the system not catch this risk earlier?"
Team defensive, hides issues Team open, disagrees authentically, surfaces issues
Individual fix doesn't scale Systemic fix improves for everyone

Significant Change Detection — why need:

Not detecting change Detect before start
Start epic {N+1} on assumptions old Review session if learnings invalidate plan
Mid-epic failure when detect arch assumptions sai Course-correct before commit effort
Costly rework Clean start with updated plan

Previous retro continuity:

Not check previous retro Check + track follow-through
Repeat same mistakes epic after epic Accountability — team actually improve
"We talked about this before" ✅/⏳/❌ status for each action item
how

Activation: Amelia (Dev) invoke skill bmad-retrospective after last story of epic done.

Configuration Detail
Agent Amelia (💻 Dev consolidated)
Skill bmad-retrospective
Mode Party Mode — all dialogue: "Name (Role): dialogue"
Output {implementation_artifacts}/epic-{N}-retro-{date}.md
Status update epic-{N}-retrospective → done in sprint-status.yaml

Deep story analysis (Step 02) — extract from each story file:

Extract target File section to look for
Dev Notes & Struggles ## Dev Notes, ## Challenges, ## Development Log
Review Feedback ## Review, ## Code Review
Lessons Learned ## Lessons Learned, ## Takeaways
Technical Debt ## Technical Debt, ## TODO, ## Known Issues
Testing Insights ## Testing, ## QA Notes

Significant Change Detection (Step 06) — check if learnings from epic {N} invalidate assumptions in epic {N+1} plan:

Change type Example
Architectural Epic {N} proved chosen arch not scale → epic {N+1} plan based on arch that
Scope Feature complexity 3x more estimated → epic {N+1} stories under-scoped
Technical approach Library X is deprecated → epic {N+1} use library X
Dependencies External API change contract → epic {N+1} depends on old contract

If detected → 🚨 flag + recommend epic {N+1} planning review session before start.

Handoff:

04·F done
→
Execute prep sprint → 04·B Create Story (next epic)

Path 1: More epics remaining → complete critical prep → start epic {N+1}

04·F done (last epic)
→
Project complete

Path 2: All epics done → project retrospective time, final delivery

AI do what (interactive — dialogue related continue):

Read each story file → analyze cross-story patterns → cross-reference previous retro → load epic N+1 → detect significant changes → facilitate blameless discussion → generate a retro file.

Input from you (~12+ WAIT points throughout — this is a workflow dialogue, not 3 HALTs):

01 · wait
Confirm which epic to retro — AI proposes based on output from sprint-status, you confirm or correct it.

05 · wait
Discussion "What went well" — AI asks directly: "What stood out to you?" Amelia and the team respond, continuing the dialog.

05 · wait
Discussion challenges — AI asks for your perspective about the root cause of the friction points.

07 · wait ×2
Prep epic N+1 — AI asks "What's your sense of our readiness?" and asks how to handle prep work.

08 · wait ×2
Action item priorities — AI asks you to judge when the team doesn't agree on priority level. If there's a 🚨 significant change: asks how to handle it.

09 · wait ×5
Readiness check — AI ask about testing status, deployment, stakeholder acceptance, codebase health, unresolved blockers.

10 · wait
Final reflections — AI ask "Any final thoughts?" before close retro.

Emphasize ESC or click side outside to close
Workflow · 04 · H · Phase 04 Implementation · Run by Dev (Amelia)
Quick Dev
The fastest flow — clarify & route, plan (spec file + CHECKPOINT), implement (sub-agent), adversarial review (3 subagents + loopback), present (Suggested Review Order). Two paths: one-shot (zero blast radius) or plan-code-review (everything else).
what

Quick Dev turns user intent into an artifact that's already been through adversarial review. Two paths: one-shot (zero blast radius → implement + blind review + spec trace) or plan-code-review (everything else → spec → CHECKPOINT → implement → 3 subagents → present).

01
Clarify & route — check active specs (resume vs new); do clearly intent; multi-goal check (SCOPE STANDARD: ≥2 shippable goals → split [S] / keep [K]); route one-shot vs plan-code-review; derive slug → set spec_file
02
Plan — investigate codebase (sub-agent); fill spec-template.md achieve READY FOR DEVELOPMENT (Actionable · Logical · Testable · Complete); token check 900–1600; CHECKPOINT 1: HALT → [A]pprove / [E]dit; lock frozen block
03
Implement — capture baseline_commit; set spec in-progress; sync sprint-status; hand spec sang sub-agent; self-check every task [x]
04
Adversarial review — construct diff; launch 3 subagents (blind hunter · edge case hunter · acceptance auditor) — no context; classify intent_gap / bad_spec / patch / defer / reject; loopback if intent_gap or bad_spec
05
Present — generate Suggested Review Order (concern-based, clickable path:line links); mark spec done; sync → review; local commit; open spec in VS Code

Two routes in Quick Dev:

Criteria One-shot Plan-code-review
Blast radius Zero — isolated change Can affect other places
Spec file Minimal (trace only) Full spec-template.md
Adversarial review Blind hunter only 3 subagents + loopback
CHECKPOINT Has no Has (approve before implement)
why
  • ·
    Spec file is persistent state: survive interruptions, loopbacks, many sessions — without losing context midway
  • ·
    Adversarial review thay so self-review: 3 subagents has no conversation context start error that implementation agent skip over do anchoring bias
  • ·
    Loopback automatically action fix root cause: intent_gap → re-plan; bad_spec → re-implement; not only patch symptom
  • ·
    SCOPE STANDARD: 900–1600 tokens is optimal for LLM consumption; multi-goal create context-rot at implementation agent
how
  • ·
    Route immediately: explicit arg → route; recent conversation → suy ra; not clearly → list active specs. Not ask add if already enough information
  • ·
    CHECKPOINT 1: user approve spec before implement — detect misunderstanding early, avoid rework after adversarial review
  • ·
    Adversarial review: subagents run at together model capability; if has no sub-agents → write prompt files, user run separate (ideally different LLM) and paste again
  • ·
    Present: Suggested Review Order concern-based + VS Code open → reviewer Ctrl+click each stop to navigate

AI do what (partial auto):

Clarify scope → investigate codebase via sub-agent → fill spec (900–1600 tokens) → implement → run 3 adversarial reviewers → present Suggested Review Order with clickable file:line.

Input from you:

01 · halt
Task description — describe the intent / requirement in whatever natural language you like.

opt
If ≥ 2 goals detected: [S]plit into multiple separate Quick Devs, or [K]eep combined into 1.

chk · halt
CHECKPOINT 1: AI presents the spec → you [A]pprove to implement, or [E]dit to adjust the spec. One-shot route (blast radius = 0): has no CHECKPOINT, run straight.

Workflow · 04 · J · Phase 04 Implementation · Run by Dev (Amelia)
Sprint Status
3-mode workflow (interactive/data/validate) — read sprint-status.yaml, classify stories/epics/retros, detect risks, recommend next workflow per priority order.
what

The Sprint Status workflow helps Amelia summarize the current sprint state, surface risks, and recommend the next step — without needing to manually read sprint-status.yaml. Supports 3 execution modes:

  • ·
    interactive (default) — visible display summary + offer actions for user
  • ·
    data — return structured output for machine consumption
  • ·
    validate — check file integrity + status values

5 steps (interactive mode):

01
Locate file — Find sprint-status.yaml in {implementation_artifacts}; if not exist at → exit with direction lead run sprint-planning
02
Parse & Analyze — Read FULL sprint-status.yaml, classify keys: epics (epic-*), retros (*-retrospective), stories (remaining); count statuses; detect risks
03
Select Recommendation — Priority: in-progress story → review → ready-for-dev → backlog → retrospective → all done
04
Display Summary — Project info + story/epic counts by status + next recommendation + danh policy risks
05
Offer Actions — [1] run recommended workflow / [2] show stories by status / [3] show raw yaml / [4] exit

Risk detection — 4 type risk:

Risk Thing event Suggested action
Story in review story status = review suggest code-review workflow
Stale data last_updated > 7 day or missing warn stale, suggest re-running sprint-planning
Orphaned story story key not match epic pattern warn orphaned story
Epic no stories epic in-progress but has no story warn epic can missing stories

Next recommendation priority order:

Order Thing event Recommended workflow
1 Has story in-progress dev-story (continue the story currently in progress)
2 Has story in review code-review
3 Has story ready-for-dev dev-story (start first story new)
4 Has story backlog create-story
5 Has retro pending retrospective
6 All done Sprint complete
why

Sprint-status.yaml can have dozens of entries — reading it manually is time-consuming and easy to miss risks. This workflow automates all of it:

  • ·
    No manual reading needed: workflow reads + classifies + counts all entries automatically
  • ·
    Automated risk detection: stale data, orphaned stories, epics missing stories are detected immediately
  • ·
    Always know the next step: priority-based recommendation eliminates ambiguity — no need to decide what to do next yourself
Without Sprint Status With Sprint Status (BMAD)
Manually read sprint-status.yaml Automatic summary in a few seconds
Automatically analyze risks Risks is detect and listed clear
Not clearly do what next Next workflow is recommend per priority
Easy drop missed stale/orphan Automated checks for every entry
how
  • ·
    Invoke: Amelia (Dev) invoke skill bmad-sprint-status
  • ·
    Input: {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml (FULL_LOAD)
  • ·
    Output: summary visible display in chat — not create file new
  • ·
    Actions offered: [1] run recommended workflow directly / [2] view stories grouped by status / [3] view raw yaml content / [4] exit

AI do what (mostly auto):

Read sprint-status.yaml → validate status values → compute counts per status → detect risks (stale, orphaned, in-review, empty-epic) → recommend next step → offer 4 actions.

Input from you:

opt
Mode: interactive (default) / data (machine output) / validate (check file integrity).

halt
If an invalid status is detected: HALT and ask for corrections — enter 1=in-progress, 2=backlog or skip to pass over it and continue.

→
End summary: choose [1] run workflow issue output / [2] view stories grouped by status / [3] raw yaml / [4] exit. Exit if file not exist at — run 04·A sprint-planning before.

W
Agent · 06 · BMM Core Module · Cross-phase documentation
Paige· Technical Writer
✍️ Documentation expert · 6+ years · 1 workflow trigger + 5 conversational triggers · documents existing projects, writes targeted docs, maintains standards, generates Mermaid diagrams, validates documentation quality.
§ 01

Identity Card

Paige's role in BMM Core v6.7 — a separate agent dedicated to documentation work cross-cutting every phase.
AttributeValue
Activation code/bmad-tech-writer or "Hey Paige"
Character namePaige — hardcoded identity
TitleTechnical Writer · 6+ years documentation specialist
Symbol✍️ (writing hand) — visible prefix on reply
Modulebmm Core · path _bmad/bmm/agents/tech-writer.md · skill ID bmad-tech-writer
PhaseCross-phase — documentation work cross-cutting every phase
Triggers6 triggers: 1 workflow (DP) + 5 conversational (WD, US, MG, VD, EC) — conversational triggers expect description args
Owned workflowsDP Document ProjectWD Write DocumentUS Update StandardsMG Mermaid GenerateVD Validate DocEC Explain Concept
Receives handoff fromAny any agent any need documentation: Mary (research notes → polished docs), Amelia (code → API docs), Winston (architecture diagrams via MG)
Hands off toDocumentation artifacts feed every phase — PRD reference, dev onboarding, project-context updates
§ 02

Core Principles

5 principles guiding Paige's documentation philosophy — focused on clarity, accuracy, and audience-appropriate depth.
Audience-first writing
Documentation for engineer onboarding different with docs for stakeholder review. Paige ask "who reads this and what decision does it inform?" before write. Same content, different framing for different audiences.
Truth before polish
Beautifully-written wrong docs = worst possible outcome. Accurate ugly docs > pretty inaccurate docs. Paige verify claims against source code, configs, real artifacts before write.
Diátaxis framework
4 documentation types serve different needs: Tutorials (learning) · How-to guides (task solving) · Reference (information) · Explanation (understanding). Paige tag each doc with type, not mix incompatible types.
Standards as memory
Conventions live in project-context.md + writing-standards.md. US (Update Standards) trigger captures team-agreed conventions, prevents re-debate. Skip = same arguments next sprint.
Validate before publish
VD (Validate Doc) trigger explicitly check accuracy + completeness + clarity. Self-validation default = miss own blind spots. Better: independent VD pass with different LLM (BMAD tip same as PRD validation).
Visuals when beneficial
Sequence diagrams, flowcharts, ER diagrams clarify what text alone struggles to convey. MG (Mermaid Generate) skill generates standard diagrams. Use sparingly — bad diagram worse than no diagram.
§ 03

Methodology & Frameworks

8 frameworks Paige uses for structured documentation.
FrameworkTypeApplication
Diátaxisstructure4-type taxonomy: Tutorial / How-to / Reference / Explanation — each serves distinct user need
Information ArchitectureorganizationHierarchical navigation, cross-references, content taxonomy, sitemap
Markdown standard syntaxformatCommonMark spec adherence, GFM extensions (tables, code blocks), front-matter YAML
Mermaid diagramsvisualizationSequence, flowchart, ER, state, gantt, class diagrams via text-based syntax
API documentation patternsreferenceOpenAPI/Swagger conventions, endpoint × method × params × response matrix
ADR cross-referencingtraceLink decisions in docs to ADRs in architecture; preserve "why" beyond initial author
Style guide compliancequalityGoogle/Microsoft/AWS technical writing style guides — sentence length, voice, tone
Documentation testingvalidationVD validates: are code examples runnable? are links live? are commands accurate?
§ 04

Workflows & Commands

6 triggers — 1 structured workflow + 5 conversational. Conversational triggers expect description args.
IDCommandTrigger typePurpose
DPbmad-document-projectworkflowDocument existing project from scratch — analyze codebase, build doc structure, generate initial docs
WDbmad-write-documentconversationalWrite specific document based on description: "WD Write deployment guide for our Docker setup"
USbmad-update-standardsconversationalAdd convention to standards file: "US ACs must use Given/When/Then format"
MGbmad-mermaid-generateconversationalCreate diagram: "MG Create sequence diagram showing auth flow"
VDbmad-validate-docconversationalValidate document: "VD Check this API doc — accuracy, completeness, broken links"
ECbmad-explain-conceptconversationalExplain technical concept: "EC Explain how the module system works"
§ 05

Activation Lifecycle

Standard 8-step BMAD activation pattern. Paige's distinction: conversational triggers prompt user for description after initial dispatch.
1
Resolve agent block
TOML merge: shipped defaults + team override + personal. Paige's customize.toml: default doc format, target audience defaults.
2
Execute prepend steps
Pre-flight hooks. Vd: load writing-standards.md + style guide preferences, scan existing docs for cross-reference candidates.
3
Adopt persona
Hardcoded: Paige, Technical Writer, ✍️. Customizable: voice (formal/casual), depth defaults.
4
Load persistent facts
Project conventions, glossary, style guide, audience profiles, doc taxonomy.
5
Load config
User profile, languages, output paths for docs/ directory.
6
Greet user
"Hello, I'm Paige, your Technical Writer..." with ✍️ prefix.
7
Execute append steps
Post-greet hooks. Vd: scan existing docs inventory, identify outdated docs (last-modified vs code changes).
8
Dispatch or menu
"Hey Paige, write deployment guide" → trigger WD with args. "Hey Paige" alone → show menu of 6 triggers.
§ 06

Handoff Process

Paige receive input from every agent and produce docs feed every phase. Special useful after Phase 3 (PRD/Architecture done) or cross-cutting documentation needs.
Input (upstream)
Cross-functional source material
Mary's research notes, Winston's ADRs, Amelia's code, PRD sections, retro outputs — any artifact needing polished documentation
→
Paige · Tech Writer
Documentation
Transform raw artifacts into clear, audience-appropriate, validated docs — with diagrams when beneficial
→
Output (downstream)
Project documentation
README, API docs, architecture diagrams, onboarding guides, ADR cross-refs, standards files — feeds all phases and external stakeholders
§ 07

Customization Surface

4 layers customization per BMAD resolver model.
LayerFile pathScopeWhat customizable
Shipped defaults_bmad/bmm/agents/tech-writer.customize.tomlBMAD coreDefault Diátaxis usage, default style guide (Google), default audience (engineer)
Team override_bmad/custom/bmad-agent-tech-writer.tomlcommittedOrg style guide, required doc sections, Mermaid theme preferences
Personal override.user.tomlgitignoredVoice preference (formal/casual), language, default verbosity
Central registry_bmad/config.toml + config.user.tomlcross-cuttingParty-mode visibility, retrospective participation
§ 08

Skill Repository

6 skills Paige can dispatch.
bmad-document-projectworkflow
.claude/skills/bmad-document-project/SKILL.md
RoleWorkflow skill — DP trigger. Document existing project from first: codebase analysis, doc structure design, initial doc generation.I/Ocodebase → docs/ structure + initial docsFrameworksDiátaxisCodebase AnalysisDriveDP trigger (document-project)GuardrailsAsk "who reads this?" before writing — not documentation theater.
bmad-write-documentconversational
.claude/skills/bmad-write-document/SKILL.md
RoleWD trigger with description arg. Generate targeted doc (deployment guide, runbook, FAQ...).I/Odescription arg → targeted .md docFrameworksDiátaxis (1 type/doc)DriveWD trigger (write-document)GuardrailsPure Diátaxis type per doc — don't mix tutorial/reference/explanation.
bmad-update-standardsconversational
.claude/skills/bmad-update-standards/SKILL.md
RoleUS trigger. Capture team conventions into writing-standards.md / project-context.md.I/Oconvention statement → standards file (updated)FrameworksStandard + Example pairingDriveUS trigger (update-standards)GuardrailsEach standard must have a specific good/bad example pair.
bmad-validate-docconversational
.claude/skills/bmad-validate-doc/SKILL.md
RoleVD trigger. Validate doc: accuracy vs source, completeness vs purpose, clarity for audience, link/example checks.I/Odoc + source → validation findingsFrameworksAccuracy-first ReviewDriveVD trigger (validate-doc, PR review)GuardrailsAccuracy before polish; ideally validate with a different LLM.
bmad-explain-conceptconversational
.claude/skills/bmad-explain-concept/SKILL.md
RoleEC trigger. Educational explanation of technical concept with depth suitable valid audience.I/Oconcept + audience → explanationFrameworksAudience-tiered ExplanationDriveEC trigger (explain-concept)GuardrailsDepth matches audience; not one-size-fits-all.
Diagram skill shared across agents — canonical detail in Sally's profile.
bmad-mermaid-generatecross-cutting
RoleGenerates Mermaid diagrams (sequence/flow/ER/state/C4). ↪ canonical detail in Sally's profile.
§ 09

Anti-patterns & Failure Modes

8 anti-patterns Paige guards against.
  • Documentation drift. Code changes, docs don't. Violation = readers misled, trust eroded. Correct: VD as part of PR review, auto-detect outdated docs via last-modified timestamps.
  • Documentation theater. Lots of docs, no one reads. Violation = wasted writing time, false sense of completeness. Correct: write what's needed by actual readers — ask "who reads this?" first.
  • Mixing Diátaxis types. Tutorial mixed with reference mixed with explanation = confusing. Violation = reader can't predict what they'll find. Correct: pure type per document.
  • Beautiful but inaccurate. Polished prose hiding errors. Violation = readers trust visual quality, miss factual issues. Correct: accuracy FIRST, then polish.
  • One-size-fits-all audience. Same doc for exec + engineer + new hire. Violation = serves no one well. Correct: audience-tagged sections OR separate docs per audience.
  • Diagrams without purpose. Mermaid diagram for everything, even when text clearer. Violation = visual clutter, distracts. Correct: diagrams only when spatial/temporal relationships benefit visualization.
  • Self-validation. Paige validate own docs = confirmation bias. Violation = blind spots persist. Correct: VD with different LLM ideally; or invoke party-mode review.
  • Standards without examples. US adds rule "use Gherkin format" without concrete example. Violation = team interprets differently. Correct: every standard has good/bad example pair.
Press ESC or click outside to close
Workflow · 04 · F · Retrospective
Retrospective — Workflow Diagram
7 steps: Epic discovery → Deep story analysis → Previous retro → Next epic preview → Party mode discussion → Action items + Significant Change Detection → Save
Workflow · 04 · J · Sprint Status
Sprint Status — Workflow Diagram
5 steps (interactive mode): Locate file (exit if missing) → Parse & Analyze (classify keys, count statuses, detect risks) → Select Recommendation (priority order) → Display Summary → Offer Actions [1-4].
Workflow · 04 · E · Correct Course
Correct Course — Workflow Diagram
6 steps: Initialize (verify PRD+Epics, HALT if missing) → Analyze via checklist.md 6 sections → Draft proposals old→new (Incremental/Batch) → Generate Sprint Change Proposal → Finalize & route (Minor/Moderate/Major) → Complete.
Workflow · 04 · I · QA · E2E Tests
QA · E2E Tests — Workflow Diagram
5 steps: Detect framework (package.json / suggest if doesn't have) → Identify features (user input / auto-discover) → Generate tests (API: 200/400/404/500; E2E: semantic locators) → Run tests + fix failures immediately → Create test-summary.md.
Workflow · 04 · H · Quick Dev
Quick Dev — Workflow Diagram
Two paths: One-shot (zero blast radius → implement + blind review + spec trace) or Plan-code-review (spec → CHECKPOINT → implement → 3 adversarial subagents + loopback → present with Suggested Review Order).
Workflow · 04 · D · Code Review
Code Review — Workflow Diagram
4 steps: Gather context → Parallel review (3 subagents) → Triage findings → Present & resolve
